The prominence began with Samuel Alexander (1815–1878), who was instrumental not only economically but culturally and socially within Zagreb. He founded the city’s first Jewish school, established several philanthropic organizations including a hospital for poor children which still exists today as part of University Hospital Center Sestre milosrdnice in its modern form. Samuel's eldest son Mihajlo (1848–1919) became one notable figure among them; he was an influential industrialist and banker. His efforts contributed significantly to the development industry, notably founding “Sava” Insurance Company which remains active today as SAVA d.d., a major insurance provider in Croatia. The Alexanders were also known for their patronage of arts: they supported many cultural initiatives such as opera houses or theaters within Zagreb—enhancing its reputation both locally and internationally. Their contributions to public works included funding parts necessary infrastructure projects like the construction bridges, which facilitated commerce across Dalmatian cities along Adriatic coast. Here are some general guidelines to follow if you want only customers from one version of your store (Magento CE) or another(magento EE): 1.) Identify the necessary tables for customer information in both versions, such us `customer_entity`, and so on; 2.). Use a tool like SQLyog which allows exporting data directly into CSV format. This will allow you to manually select only customers from one version of your store or another without having any other unnecessary fields included with them (like orders); 3.) Note that you may want to configure the maximum connections per route because as per RFC 2616 spec HttpAsyncClient will only allow a maximum of two concurrent connections to the same host by default. And there are plenty of other options that you can configure as shown in HttpAsyncClient configuration. HttpAsyncClient is expensive to create, therefore you do not want to create an instace of it on each GET operation. One listener is hooked to the AsyncContext, this listener is only used in the example above to handle timeouts. public class ResponseListener implements AsyncListener { @Override public void onStartAsync(AsyncEvent event) throws IOException { } @Override public void onComplete(AsyncEvent event) throws IOException { } @Override public void onError(AsyncEvent event) throws IOException { event.getAsyncContext().getResponse().getWriter().print("error:"); } @Override public void onTimeout(AsyncEvent event) throws IOException { event.getAsyncContext().getResponse().getWriter().print("timeout:"); } } Then you need a consumer for the HTTP client. texts.full_text: er for lipid lowering? This is a critical debate, as emerging data from the Heart Protection Study (HPS), a large trial involving over 20,000 patients with diabetes or high-risk conditions, demonstrated significant cardiovascular risk reduction with statin therapy even in patients with relatively normal LDL cholesterol levels. This suggests that lower LDL targets may be beneficial, but the optimal threshold remains uncertain. Current guidelines, such as those from the American Diabetes Association (ADA), recommend LDL cholesterol targets <100 mg/dL for most patients, though individualization is key. Ongoing trials like the Collaborative AtoRvastatin Diabetes Study (CARDS) and Atorvastatin Study for Prevention of Endpoints in NIDDM (ASPEN) aim to clarify these questions. Early results from these trials showed that intensive statin therapy reduced the risk of cardiovascular events in patients with type 2 diabetes, even in the absence of prior heart disease. However, the long-term benefits of targeting LDL levels far below 70 mg/dL—commonly achieved with high-dose statins—remain under investigation, as do potential harms such as increased incidence of diabetes in some non-diabetic populations observed with very low LDL levels. Another evolving area is the role of combination therapies. While statins remain the cornerstone, adding agents like ezetimibe, PCSK9 inhibitors, or fibrates (for triglyceride-rich dyslipidaemia) may further reduce residual risk. The recent ACCORD Lipid trial, which tested adding fenofibrate to statin therapy, showed no additional benefit for cardiovascular outcomes, but this may reflect suboptimal dosing or patient selection. Future trials may focus on integrating novel biomarkers (e.g., coronary artery calcium scoring) and individualized risk stratification to tailor lipid-lowering strategies. In summary, the future of diabetic dyslipidaemia management likely involves (1) aggressive, guideline-recommended LDL lowering with statins as primary prevention, (2) a nuanced approach to "lower is better" by balancing benefits against risks of over-treatment, and (3) combination therapies targeting non-LDL components (e.g., triglycerides, HDL) in high-risk patients. texts.full_text: Favre, Jr., Asst. U. S. Atty., Mobile, Ala., for appellee. Before TJOFLAT, VANCE and HATCHETT, Circuit Judges. ### Opinion #### Background: This case arises from the conviction of James Morrow Roper (Roper), Christian Matthew Newton (Newton), and John Jackson Miller Truxell (Truxell) for conspiracy to possess with intent to distribute marijuana in violation of 21 U.S.C. § 846, possession with intent to distribute approximately five tons of marijuana on a vessel subject to the jurisdiction of the United States under 21 U.S.C. §§ 955 and 955a(a), (c)(3)–(d). The defendants were also convicted for using or maintaining an aircraft in connection with drug trafficking activities, violating § 846. #### Facts: On March 7, 1980, the United States Coast Guard intercepted a vessel named "Sea Witch" approximately fifty miles south of Dauphin Island, Alabama. Upon boarding and searching the boat, agents discovered over five tons (approximately ten thousand pounds) of marijuana hidden in various compartments on board. The defendants were identified through surveillance conducted by federal authorities prior to this interception. Evidence presented at trial included intercepted communications between Roper and Newton discussing drug transactions involving large quantities of marijuana intended for distribution within Alabama and other states. #### Procedural History: Following a jury verdict finding all three men guilty, the district court sentenced them accordingly: each defendant received sentences ranging from 15 to 20 years imprisonment. The defendants appealed their convictions on several grounds including alleged errors in evidentiary rulings by the trial judge concerning wiretap evidence and assertions of improper venue. #### Issues: The primary issues before this Court include whether (a) certain intercepted communications were properly admitted into evidence; (b) if there was sufficient proof to establish that Alabama, where some preparatory acts occurred but not all substantive offenses took place, could serve as a proper forum for prosecution under the relevant statutes and case law governing venue in conspiracy cases. #

In order to prepare a high .alpha.-glycosyl-L-ascorbic acid content product from the reaction mixture, adsorption and desorption using an anion-exchange resin is used, and column chromatography using a cation-exchange resin have been known. The adsorption and desorption is a purification method which comprises demineralizing a reaction mixture with a cation-exchange resin (H.sup.+ -form), allowing the resultant demineralized solution to adsorb on an anion-exchange resin (OH.sup.- -form), washing the anion-exchange resin with water to remove saccharides such as D-glucose and .alpha.-glucosyl saccharide, and eluting the substances adsorbed on the anion-exchange resin with an acid- or salt-solution. The method, however, has drawbacks in that it has a complicated step and requires relatively-large amounts of water, eluate and agent for regeneration; and that the concentration and the yield of the resultant .alpha.-glycosyl-L-ascorbic acid are relatively low. texts.full_text: an employer chooses to keep a replacement employee rather than permit a striking employee to return to work. Our holding in this case is in accordance with the reasoning in Penflex. In Penflex, the supreme court, in holding that a strike which violated the notice requirements of Section 8 of the National Labor Relations Act[2] was not willful misconduct under Section 402(e) of the Unemployment Compensation Law,[3] discussed the relationship between sections 402(d) and 402(e). The supreme court noted that section 402(d) only applies to ongoing employer/employee relationships. When the relationship is severed, the employee no longer has an interest in the outcome of the labor dispute and therefore, section 402(d) is inapplicable. Penflex, 506 Pa. at 287-88, 485 A.2d at 366. Accordingly, the order of the Unemployment Compensation Board of Review is affirmed in so far as it grants unemployment compensation benefits to George Horvath after March 27, 1985. No one does it like the Yankees, nobody. The #51 is officially retired and the plaque is made for Monument Park so now it's time to win the game in true Bernie fashion, by any means Tonight the Yankees play host to the Rangers inside Yankee Stadium for the final time in this three game series with Chris Capuano and Yovani Gallardo on the mound for their respected teams. The game will be played 7:05pm ET and can be seen live on YES Network or MLB TV if you're not a New York local. Pitching Matchup * CJP - I think this lineup is good to go as long as McCann starts at first base, but the question remains who gets left off? You have Arod/Tex in the middle of that order and then Beltran batting 5th which means it's going against a right-handed pitcher. This may not be our best possible lineup with all things considered so I think we should go to Gardner at leadoff, followed by Ellsbury who has had some great AB's when leading off in the past while Arod moves up an order spot and Tex stays where he is because it seems like 90% of his walks come after someone reaches base. With Chase Headley injured there really isn't a better option than Beltran batting fifth, so let him mash against Gallardo who hasn't pitched great as far as strikeouts go this year but has been able to induce ground balls at an alarming rate with that said I think we need Gardner and Ellsbury leading off because those two will have the be

texts.full_text: Center) and two stakeholders. Legal document related to VAW was reviewed to measure up the role of the primary healthcare physicians. Our study revealed that the role of physicians in primary healthcare centers on the VAW intervention program was limited. This was due to the insufficient knowledge of the physicians on the VAW program, physicians' constraint on counseling skill, unsupportive infrastructure, and a limited number of physicians in Puskesmas. Some barriers related to the VAW program management were also discovered and needed intervention at the decision-maker level. The role of primary healthcare physicians in the comprehensive intervention of the VAW program is not optimum. texts.full_text: Thermal conversion of core-shell metal-organic frameworks: a new method for selectively functionalized nanoporous hybrid carbon. Core-shell structured ZIF-8@ZIF-67 crystals are well-designed and prepared through a seed-mediated growth method. After thermal treatment of ZIF-8@ZIF-67 crystals, we obtain selectively functionalized nanoporous hybrid carbon materials consisting of nitrogen-doped carbon (NC) as the cores and highly graphitic carbon (GC) as the shells. This is the first example of the integration of NC and GC in one particle at the nanometer level. texts.full_text: eins recognition strategies of E3 ubiquitin ligases and diseases. Impairment in the clearance of misfolded proteins by proteins leads to various late-onset neurodegenerative diseases. Cell applies a strict quality control mechanism against malfunctioned proteins which may generate toxicity within cells. E3 ubiquitin ligases play a role in this quality control process by recognizing and targeting misfolded proteins for degradation via the ubiquitin-proteasome system (UPS). Here are some strategies employed by E3 ubiquitin ligases to recognize misfolded proteins, along with their implications in neurodegenerative diseases: 1. Recognition of Abnormal Structures: E3 ligases can directly recognize abnormal structural features exposed on misfolded proteins, such as hydrophobic patches or unstructured regions. For instance, CHIP (Carboxyl terminus of Hsc70-interacting protein) is an E3 ligase that interacts with chaperones like Hsp70 and Hsp90 to ubiquitinate misfolded proteins, facilitating their degradation. 2. Chaperone-Assisted Recognition: Many E3 ligases cooperate with chaperones to identify misfolded proteins. Chaperones bind to exposed hydrophobic regions of misfolded proteins and then recruit E3 ligases for ubiquitination. This mechanism is employed by Parkin, an E3 ligase implicated in Parkinson's disease, which works in concert with chaperones like Hsp70. 3. Recognition of Specific Degrons: Some E3 ligases recognize specific degradation signals (degrons) on misfolded proteins. texts.full_text: their training in Greece, identify weaknesses and propose solutions for improvement. All ophthalmology residents in Greece during the academic year 2014–2015 were invited to participate in this study (n = 169). A structured questionnaire was developed and distributed online to all participants assessing demographics, workload, training experience, scientific background, clinical and surgical skills, as well as overall satisfaction. Descriptive statistics were used for the analysis of the results. [RESULTS]{.smallcaps} {#sec1-3} ===================== A total of 129 residents (response rate: 76.3%) completed the questionnaire. Most participants were women (n = 85, 65.9%), and the mean age was 30.4 ± 3.9 years. The majority of residents reported a heavy workload with little free time for studying or conducting research (mean working hours: 51.2 ± 11.7 h/week). Regarding training experience, most residents were satisfied with the quality and quantity of clinical practice (86.0% and 84.5%, respectively), but only 39.5% reported being satisfied with their surgical training. Almost half of the participants (47.3%) stated that they had to perform procedures without any prior training, while a significant proportion (42.6%) felt unprepared for independent practice after residency completion. [DISCUSSION]{.smallcaps} {#sec10-1} ======================= Our study provides valuable insights into the current state of ophthalmology residency training in Greece during the economic crisis. The high response rate and the large sample size increase the generalizability of our findings. The results indicate that residents are generally satisfied with their clinical training, which is a positive aspect given the challenging working conditions they face due to understaffing and heavy workloads. However, there are significant concerns regarding surgical training, with many residents feeling unprepared for independent practice. texts.full_text: present invention relates to a tester for suitably testing the connection between a trip unit and an actuatoR within a circuit breaker. A circuit breaker typically includes a pair of electrical contacts that, when closed, allows circuit current to pass to a protected portion of an electrical distribution circuit via the closed contacts. When the contacts open, circuit current is prevented from flowing from one contact member to the other, and therefore, circuit current is prevented from flowing to the protected portion of the distribution circuit. The use of electronic trip units (trip units) in such circuit breakers is well known. An electronic trip unit typically comprises voltage and current sensors, which provide analog signals indicative of the current flowing through the circuit breakerand any voltages present within it. texts.full_text: n the growth and DNA synthesis of PC12 cells. We investigated the effects of ouabain and serum from salt-loaded Dahl salt-sensitive (S) rats, which contain abundant ouabain-like compounds, on the growth and DNA synthesis of rat pheochromocytoma PC12 cells. Ouabain decreased the growth of PC12 cells, as evaluated by 3-[4,5-dimethylthiazol-2-yl]-2,5-diphenyltetrazolium bromide assay, in a concentration-dependent fashion. A moderate concentration (10(-7) M) of ouabain increased DNA synthesis, as measured by [3H] thymidine incorporation into cellular DNA; however, higher concentrations (> 10^-6 M) inhibited this process significantly. Serum from salt-loaded Dahl S rats also reduced the growth and stimulated or suppressed PC12 cell proliferation depending on its concentration. This effect was similar to that of ouabain alone but more pronounced at lower doses due possibly to a combination with other bioactive substances present in serum, such as angiotensin II. The results suggest an important role for endogenous cardiac glycosides

texts.full_text: a nitride semiconductor substrate. Recently various researches have been conducted on the growth of nitride semiconductor on a substrate made of a different material such as sapphire, spinel or silicon carbide which has a lattice constant different from that of the nitride semiconductor. For example, a method of growing epitaxial lateral overgrowth GaN (ELOG) is described in JPN. J. PAT. No. 3026089. This method uses a SiO2 mask and a nitride semiconductor layer grown on the substrate to form an inclined surface. However, this method has limitations as it requires a complex process for forming the mask and may result in crystal defects due to the lattice mismatch between the nitride semiconductor and the substrate. Another approach is described in JPN. J. PAT. No. 3698721, where a buffer layer with a superlattice structure is formed on the substrate before growing the nitride semiconductor. While this method can reduce crystal defects to some extent, it still faces challenges due to the difference in lattice constants between the substrate and the nitride semiconductor. To address these issues, the present invention provides an improved method for growing a high-quality nitride semiconductor layer on a foreign substrate with a different lattice constant. The method involves forming a buffer layer having a composition gradient of InxAlyGa1-x-yN (0≤X, 0≤Y, X+Y≤1) on the substrate and then growing the nitride semiconductor layer on the buffer layer. By gradually changing the composition of the buffer layer, the lattice mismatch between the substrate and the nitride semiconductor can be effectively mitigated, leading to reduced crystal defects and improved material q

texts.full_text: - 9 April 1911) was Archdeacon of Taunton from 1903 until his death. Askwith was educated at Cheltenham College and Trinity College, Cambridge. He was ordained in 1867 and began his career with curacies at Tidcombe then New Radford. He was Vicar of Christ Christ Church, Taunton, from 1876 until 1903, when he was appointed Archdeacon of Taunton. He was also a Canon of the Cathedral Church of St. Andrew and St. Gregory the Great, Taunton, from 1876 until his death. During his time as Archdeacon, Askwith was known for his dedication to the education and welfare of the people of Taunton. texts.full_text: articulating the immigrant experience in a nonnative language, Vapnyar and her literary cohorts are frequently called “translingual,” and explore “what it means to be a Russian writer with a hyphen." Thus, the trend for recent Russian-American authors is not to depict “immigrants on their way to ultimate assimilation…[but instead] to present themselves as partially alienated strangers” for a distinctly American audience. On the one hand, authors have “addressed the pitfalls of writing about one's country of origin for a foreign audience." For example, in “The Writer as Tour Guide,” Vapnyar “reports that a reader, who was himself a Russian immigrant, told her that her books made him uncomfortable because they were so obviously written with an American audience in mind." On the other hand, depicting “North American contexts from the perspective of a Russian newcomer” allows the authors to effectively communicate the alienating experience of immigration and to help them establish their own translingual, transcultural identities. This last point, that the act of creative writing is cathartic and formative, is especially true for Vapnyar, who has wondered (in interviews) whether she would have learned to feel at ease in the United States without writing fiction in English. For Vapnyar, creative writing is her primary “means of establishing her identity in her homeland." Vapnyar’s reliance on creative writing as a means to form her own identity is reflected in her frequent use of a trope—common to all the Fourth Wave writers—that has been defined as the “self-portrait of the author as a translingual and transcultural storyteller." texts.full_text: used, a pound is often referred to as a kilogram (kg), which equals 1000 grams. For instance, in Canada, one pound can be sold for around CAD 500-600, while in Germany, it might fetch EUR 400-500 per kg. In contrast, countries like the United Kingdom and Ireland still use the imperial system, where a pound is equivalent to 16 ounces or approximately 454 grams. Here, a pound of cannabis could cost anywhere between GBP 2000 to 3000 or EUR 2500 to 3500 depending on the strain and quality. Despite these variations, the global trend leans towards the metric system, making kilograms the standard unit for bulk purchases in most parts of the world. The decimal system of measurement is known as the International System of Units (SI). In short, cannabis is measured by grams no matter where you go. Beyond grams, cannabis weights outside the U.S. are typically referred to in kilograms, or 1,000 grams. One kilo , as

texts.full_text: that provide electrical stimulation. Electrical stimulation may be delivered to sacral, pudendal or other pelvic nerves within the pelvic floor of a patient to alleviate a variety of disorders such as urinary incontinence, fecal incontinence, constipation, sexual dysfunction, pelvic pain, or other pelvic floor disorders. Other applications include spinal cord stimulation, gastric stimulation and deep brain stimulation. The electrical stimulation is delivered via electrodes disposed at or near the distal ends of one or more leads. Leads may also be used with implantable cardiac pacemakers that provide therapeutic stimulation to the heart by delivering pacing, cardioversion or defibrillation pulation signals directly thereto.
